meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve 'No Project Ideas'
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no project ideas' trap with our SaaS starter kit guide. Build faster using Antigravity and Claude Code for intermediate-level results.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' paradox stems from overthinking market fit before building. Solve this by deploying a high-velocity SaaS starter kit. Focus on micro-SaaS tools that solve one specific pain point for a niche audience. Use Antigravity for rapid architecture and Claude Code to iterate your MVP in hours, not weeks.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You have the technical skills, the stack, and the drive, yet you sit paralyzed by the search for the 'perfect' idea. This is the founder’s paradox: waiting for inspiration while the market screams for solutions to mundane, profitable problems.

It is time to stop waiting for lightning to strike. By leveraging a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you transform the development process from a chore into a rapid deployment machine. We are stripping away the friction, allowing you to ship, validate, and pivot before your competitors even finish their wireframes.

steps.title: Define the Micro-Niche
steps.description: Identify a small, underserved subset of a larger industry where you can automate a manual task. Why? Because niche products have lower CAC and higher conversion.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a market analyst. Generate 5 micro-SaaS ideas for the logistics industry that solve specific, high-friction manual data entry tasks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Analyze the top 3 ideas and outline the primary database schema for a Postgres MVP.'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Starter Kit
steps.description: Initialize your codebase with a pre-configured SaaS boilerplate to handle Auth, Stripe, and Database ORM immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Next.js 14 project using an authentication-first architecture with Supabase and Stripe integration pre-wired.

steps.title: Build the Core Utility
steps.description: Focus strictly on the 'Value-Add' feature that solves the user's problem. Do not build dashboards or settings yet.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a clean, responsive server component that consumes the user's input and processes it through an OpenAI function call.
steps.claude_code_command: claude generate component --name CoreProcessor --logic 'OpenAI API integration'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Monetization
steps.description: If people don't pay, it's a hobby, not a business. Set up a simple subscription gate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Stripe webhook handler that updates the user's database status from 'free' to 'pro' upon successful checkout.

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Push your MVP to production immediately to gain real-world feedback loops.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel-ready configuration file and a deployment checklist for a production-grade SaaS release.

pro_tips: Prioritize 'boring' problems; they have the highest willingness to pay because they represent actual business costs.
Never build a custom auth system; use Clerk or Supabase to save 40+ hours of security engineering.
Ship the MVP without a landing page first; use a simple Tally form to validate interest before writing the full app.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your payment logic only—skip testing UI components until you have paying users.
Automate your feedback loop by embedding a 'Report a Bug' button that sends data directly to your Slack channel.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, spend 80% of your time on distribution. Reach out to the first 10 users manually on LinkedIn or X, gather their feedback, and iterate the core feature based on their actual usage patterns.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for yourself and start building for the market. By following this framework, you remove the guesswork and focus purely on shipping value. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
